---
title: "RIP-seq: Top TE Targets (Normoxia and Hypoxia)"
author: "Kate"
date: "2026-05"
output: html_document
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r libraries}
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r load_data}
nor <- read_csv(here("output", "translation_categories_si3d_vs_sictrl_normoxia_1and4hr.csv"))
hyp <- read_csv(here("output", "translation_categories_si3d_vs_sictrl_hypoxia_1hr.csv"))
```

## Top 200 TE targets by LFC (padj < 0.05)

Positive `te_lfc` = eIF3d promotes translation (log2(sictrl / si3d), so positive = knockdown reduces TE).

```{r top_targets}
top_nor <- nor |>
  filter(te_padj < 0.05) |>
  arrange(desc(te_lfc)) |>
  slice_head(n = 200) |>
  dplyr::select(symbol, te_lfc, te_padj)

top_hyp <- hyp |>
  filter(te_padj < 0.05) |>
  arrange(desc(te_lfc)) |>
  slice_head(n = 200) |>
  dplyr::select(symbol, te_lfc, te_padj)

cat("Top 200 normoxia 1+4hr targets:\n")
print(top_nor, n = 200)

cat("\nTop 200 hypoxia 1hr targets:\n")
print(top_hyp, n = 200)
```
